ai.issueType: Modal open button does not update aria-expanded
ai.description: The click handler on .open-modal-btn shows the modal dialog by setting display to block, but does not set aria-expanded to true on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed that the button has expanded a dialog.

ai.issueType: Modal close button does not update aria-expanded
ai.description: The click handler on .close-btn hides the modal by setting display to none, but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to false. Screen reader users are not informed that the dialog has closed.

ai.issueType: Form submit validation does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
ai.description: When the contact form is submitted with empty fields, the validation creates visual error indicators including red borders and text content, but fails to programmatically set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid input elements. Screen reader users cannot identify which fields have failed validation.

ai.issueType: Form blur validation does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
ai.description: The blur event handler sets aria-invalid="true" when a field is empty, but never removes this attribute or sets it to false when the field contains valid data. Screen reader users may continue to hear that the field is invalid even after correcting the error.

ai.issueType: Load more button does not announce new content to screen readers
ai.description: When the load more button is activated and new items are fetched and appended to the DOM, there is no announcement via an aria-live region to inform screen reader users that additional content has been added or that the action completed successfully.

ai.issueType: Add to cart buttons do not announce cart updates
ai.description: When items are added to the cart, the cart counter text is updated visually but no announcement is made to screen reader users to confirm the item was added or to communicate the new cart total.

ai.issueType: Filter select changes not announced to screen readers
ai.description: When filter select values change, the application state updates but no announcement is made via the aria-live region to inform screen reader users that filters have been applied or that content may have changed.

ai.issueType: Tab interface lacks keyboard navigation support
ai.description: The tab interface only handles mouse click events but does not implement keyboard event handlers for arrow keys, Home, or End keys required by the WAI-ARIA tabs pattern. Keyboard-only users cannot navigate between tabs using standard expected controls.

ai.issueType: Modal dialog lacks focus management on open
ai.description: When the modal opens, focus is not programmatically moved to the modal dialog or its close button, and focus is not trapped within the modal. Keyboard users can navigate outside the modal while it remains visually open, creating a focus trap confusion.

ai.issueType: Modal close does not return focus to trigger
ai.description: When the modal is closed, focus is not returned to the button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the page navigation sequence and must manually navigate back to the trigger location.

ai.issueType: Form error messages not associated with inputs via aria-describedby
ai.description: Error message spans are created and appended to the DOM but lack unique ids and are not programmatically associated with their corresponding input fields using aria-describedby. Screen reader users cannot reliably access the error message content when navigating to invalid fields.

ai.issueType: Menu aria-expanded not initialised on page load
ai.description: The menu button does not have its aria-expanded attribute set to 'false' during initialization. Assistive technologies will not report the initial closed state of the menu control.

ai.issueType: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
ai.description: The blur handler sets aria-invalid="true" when the input is empty but never resets it to "false" when the input becomes valid. The invalid state persists incorrectly.

ai.issueType: User action result not announced via aria-live region
ai.description: Clicking add-to-cart-btn updates the cart count text but does not trigger an announcement. Screen reader users are not informed that the item was added to the cart.

ai.issueType: Tab state not initialised on page load
ai.description: The tab buttons and panels are selected via querySelector but no initialization code sets aria-selected="true" on the first tab or displays the first panel. The initial state is undefined for assistive technology.

ai.issueType: Filter change result not announced via aria-live region
ai.description: Changing a filter select updates the appState but does not announce the filter change or result update to screen readers. Users are unaware the view has changed.

lighthouse.issueType: Form elements do not have associated labels
lighthouse.description: Labels ensure that form controls are announced properly by assistive technologies, like screen readers. [Learn more about form element labels](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/label).

lighthouse.issueType: Form elements do not have associated labels
lighthouse.description: Labels ensure that form controls are announced properly by assistive technologies, like screen readers. [Learn more about form element labels](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/label).

lighthouse.description: Labels ensure that form controls are announced properly by assistive technologies, like screen readers. [Learn more about form element labels](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/label).

lighthouse.description: Labels ensure that form controls are announced properly by assistive technologies, like screen readers. [Learn more about form element labels](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/label).

lighthouse.issueType: Select elements do not have associated label elements.
lighthouse.description: Form elements without effective labels can create frustrating experiences for screen reader users. [Learn more about the `select` element](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/select-name).

lighthouse.issueType: Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing.
lighthouse.description: Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. [Learn more about touch targets](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/target-size).

lighthouse.issueType: Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing.
lighthouse.description: Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. [Learn more about touch targets](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/target-size).

lighthouse.description: Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. [Learn more about touch targets](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/target-size).
